mission.title: karmada: kubectl-karmada add `init` Command
mission.description:
**What type of PR is this?**
/kind design

**What this PR does / why we need it**:
easy and quick install of karmada
**Which issue(s) this PR fixes**:
Fixes #804

**Special notes for your reviewer**:
kubectl-karmada add `init` Command
```
# ./kubectl-karmada
kubectl karmada controls a Kubernetes Cluster Federation.

Usage:
  karmada [flags]
  karmada [command]

Available Commands:
  completion  generate the autocompletion script for the specified shell
  cordon      Mark cluster as unschedulable
  get         Display one or many resources
  help        Help about any command
  init        bootstrap install karmada (default in kubernetes)
  join        Register a cluster to control plane
  taint       Update the taints on one or more clusters.
  uncordon    Mark cluster as schedulable
  unjoin      Remove the registration of a cluster from control plane
  version     Print the version information.
```
**Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?**:

```release-note

```

resolution.summary:
Hi @RainbowMango
I added a sub-command to kubectl-karmada. If there is no problem, the new sub-commands will also be added in this form.
steps: Review the issue discussion for context
Apply the fix from the linked pull request

kubectl karmada controls a Kubernetes Cluster Federation.

Available Commands:

  uncordon    Mark cluster as schedulable
  unjoin      Remove the registration of a cluster from control plane
  version     Print the version information.

Welcome @prodanlabs! It looks like this is your first PR to karmada-io/karmada 🎉
Hi @RainbowMango
I added a sub-command to kubectl-karmada. If there is no problem, the new sub-commands will also be added in this form.
It looks good.  I was interested in this feature in #804. Thank you for implementing it.

I have considered whether we can read the crd and other object  from local file while compiling it, rather than setting it a constant like `pkg/kaadm/karmada/crds/bases_cluster_karmada_io_clusters.go`, because we possibly update these object, which could increase code maintenance costs. What do you think?

@lonelyCZ  Good idea. I'm still working on the final work of `karmada in container`, let me see if I have time later
Hi @RainbowMango trouble re-trigger `ci`
Done. Sorry for the delay.
